highlights.title: OpenAI’s Feud With xAI Carries On as Apple Secrets Fight Revs Up
highlights.description: OpenAI asked a judge to find that a lawsuit filed by xAI Corp. accusing the ChatGPT maker of trade secret theft “should never have been filed” and seeks to recoup more than $1 million in legal expenses from Elon Musk’s company.

highlights.title: Uber’s product chief on hotels, robotaxis, and why the company doesn’t want to be “everything for everyone”
highlights.description: Uber Chief Product Officer Sachin Kansal walks TechCrunch through the company's financial-services ambitions, its increasingly complicated relationship with Waymo, its new AV Labs data operation, and how AI is starting to show up in ways riders and drivers will actually notice.

highlights.title: Nvidia Partner GMI Cloud Seeks $635 Million GPU-Backed Bank Loan
highlights.description: US-based data center operator GMI Cloud is seeking a NT$20.45 billion ($635 million) multi-tranche loan deal supported by customer contracts for graphics processing units, one of the first such financings in Asia that reflects the region’s growing demand for artificial intelligence.

highlights.title: SoftBank Billionaire: Nuclear Fusion Is Key to AI Future
highlights.description: SoftBank founder Masayoshi Son said that in the not-so-distant future, nuclear fusion technology will offer the most realistic solution for powering AI data centers’ ballooning needs. He speaks at an annual SoftBank World event in Tokyo.  (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: SoftBank’s Masayoshi Son Sees Fusion as Key to AI’s Future
highlights.description: SoftBank Group Corp. founder Masayoshi Son said that in the not-so-distant future, nuclear fusion technology will offer the most realistic solution for powering AI data centers’ ballooning needs.

highlights.title: Huawei, Apple Get Boost in China From Memory Costs, IDC Says
highlights.description: Huawei Technologies Co. and Apple Inc. extended their lead atop China’s smartphone market in the June quarter, expanding their shipments as consumers opted to either buy premium devices or skip upgrades, according to IDC.

highlights.title: SpaceX is gearing up for Starship's 13th test flight later this week
highlights.description: This flight will put Starship under higher pressure and test out new Starlink satellites in orbit.

highlights.title: Google brings Gemini in Chrome to UK users
highlights.description: Chrome's Gemini integration has arrived in the UK.

highlights.title: Trillion Dollar Chip Rout Trains Spotlight on TSMC and ASML Results
highlights.description: At a time of acute investor unease over a selloff in AI-inflated tech stocks, this week’s results from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. and ASML Holding NV carry even greater significance than usual.

highlights.title: India’s Antitrust Body Fines HP $13.3 Million for Cartelizing
highlights.description: India’s antitrust regulator fined the Indian unit of HP Inc. 1.27 billion rupees ($13.3 million), saying the company colluded with its resellers to rig prices in government procurement tenders.

ai.title: South Korea Turns More Bullish on Economy as Chip Boom Rolls On
ai.description: South Korea’s government is taking a more bullish view of the economy than the International Monetary Fund’s recent assessment, maintaining that an artificial intelligence-driven semiconductor boom will continue to outweigh the impact of the Middle East conflict.

ai.title: State Street to Continue Buying Tech Dips, Veitmane Says
ai.description: Marija Veitmane, equity research head at State Street Global Markets, discusses the artificial intelligence trade and why she sees opportunities in software stocks. "We see analysts stabilizing their margin expectation revenue," Veitmane tells Bloomberg Television. "At the same time, we see institutional investors starting to buy from this big, big underweight position," she adds. (Source: Bloomberg)

ai.title: SK Hynix Shares Rebound After Early Rout as AI Jitters Persist
ai.description: SK Hynix Inc. erased an early plunge to climb more than 3% in Seoul on Tuesday, underscoring the extreme volatility gripping one of the world’s biggest beneficiaries of the artificial intelligence boom.

ai.title: Finland’s NestAI Builds Sovereign AI Tools for Europe Militaries
ai.description: Finnish artificial intelligence lab NestAI Oy is launching an AI engine that the Finnish and Estonian militaries will use to create sovereign models for planning operations and enabling uncrewed autonomous systems.

ai.title: Google rolls out first Fitbit Air firmware update [U]
ai.description: Following two big Google Health app updates, the Fitbit Air is getting its first post-launch firmware update.

crypto.title: U.S. government moves $288 million in seized bitcoin, ether to Coinbase Prime
crypto.description: Coins from the Farace and BTC-e seizures moved through fresh wallets before landing on the exchange, despite president Donald Trump's previous no-sell reserve order.
